refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into monitoring, control, and optimization clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved and verified.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify energy consumption data collection accuracy, control algorithm effectiveness, and optimization results against the target energy_per_ton metric over the evaluation period.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and workflows to achieve energy efficiency target.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and tonnage sensors with telemetry data aggregation.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to reduce energy consumption.
DP3: Use data analytics and machine learning to optimize operational parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring (DP1) and control (DP2).
Feedback loop between control (DP2) and optimization (DP3).
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and managed via well-defined telemetry and tasking interfaces.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Risk of control algorithm not adapting well to varying conditions.
Risk that optimization may conflict with safety constraints.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure safety constraints are explicitly integrated into control and optimization DPs.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level integration of energy monitoring, control, and optimization functions.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time energy and tonnage data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands from NGHE Solution to Vehicle for adaptive control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone supporting data flow among NGHE Solution, Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes energy efficiency logic and data processing; minimal diffusion.
Hosts sensors and executes control commands; some diffusion due to embedded control.
Limited role in energy efficiency; mostly remote control and monitoring.
Focus on safety alerts; minimal direct involvement in energy efficiency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency functions are best contained within the NGHE Solution module to centralize logic and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential performance bottleneck if all processing centralized.
Dependency on reliable network links for telemetry and tasking.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider distributing some control logic to Vehicle module to reduce latency while maintaining central optimization in NGHE Solution.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched with selected DPs covering monitoring, control, and optimization aspects.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion of control logic into Vehicle module may complicate ownership.
Telemetry interface critical for coupling; network reliability is a risk.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as primary owner; Vehicle module involvement is justified for control execution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data analysis and operational testing of control and optimization functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of functions to NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly incorporate safety constraints into control and optimization DPs.
Evaluate network reliability impact on telemetry and tasking interfaces.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, safety constraints need explicit integration and network reliability risks require mitigation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and workflows to achieve energy efficiency target.

revise.why: To explicitly integrate safety constraints and address network reliability in control and optimization design parameters.

assumptions: Reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Safety constraints can be integrated without compromising energy efficiency.
open_risks: Control algorithm adaptability under varying conditions.
Network reliability impacting telemetry and tasking.
Potential conflicts between safety and energy efficiency goals.
evidence_needed: Performance data of control algorithms under real conditions.
Network reliability statistics and mitigation strategies.
Safety impact analysis of energy efficiency optimizations.
